=========================
SUPERPOWER — LLM MASTER CONTEXT (V2)
=========================

Purpose:
This document provides an LLM with a complete, structured understanding of Superpower: what it is, how it works, who it serves, what biomarkers it measures, how the membership operates, and how the user experience unfolds.

It includes summaries, hierarchies, categories, definitions, and metadata, making it ideal for retrieval, reasoning, product knowledge, copywriting, support prompts, and grounding.

=====================================
1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
=====================================

Superpower is a health-intelligence membership offering 100+ biomarker lab tests once per year, delivered through CLIA-certified and CAP-accredited labs. Members receive a comprehensive blood panel, an evolving health plan, medical-team support, and access to data insights.

The focus is proactive, prevention-driven health: early detection, understanding baseline biology, tracking trajectory changes, optimizing metabolic and hormonal health, and supporting long-term resilience and performance.

Superpower consolidates testing, interpretation, education, and ongoing support into one annual experience that goes far beyond standard checkups.

=====================================
2. HOW SUPERpower WORKS
=====================================

SOURCE: superpower.com/how-it-works

2.1 Membership Model

Annual membership includes a 100+ biomarker blood test at the start of each year.

No hidden fees.

HSA/FSA eligible.

Access to physicians, health specialists, and data insights.

2.2 Process Timeline

Sign Up – Member completes onboarding, medical questionnaire, and test authorization.

Schedule Blood Draw – Either at a partner lab (e.g., Quest) or via a mobile phlebotomist (depending on region).

Lab Processing – Samples analyzed in CLIA- and CAP-accredited labs.

Results Delivered (~10 days) – Results release directly to the member dashboard.

Health Plan & Review – Personalized health plan created using biomarker insights + user lifestyle data.

Continuous Support – Concierge and medical team available year-round.

2.3 What Makes Superpower Different

Tests 5–10× more biomarkers than a standard primary care checkup.

Emphasis on root-cause, functional, metabolic, and longevity biomarkers.

Tracks biological baselines and interprets multi-system patterns rather than isolated values.

Built for preventive health, not acute or symptom-based care.

2.4 Limitations

Not intended for emergency, urgent care, or acute diagnostics.

Does not replace a primary care physician.

Availability varies by U.S. state.

=====================================
3. WHAT SUPERPOWER TESTS
=====================================

SOURCE: superpower.com/biomarkers

Superpower measures 100+ biomarkers across major physiological systems. These reveal metabolic health, hormonal balance, inflammation, organ function, nutrient status, and disease risk.

Below is a structured, LLM-friendly breakdown.

3.1 Cardiovascular & Vascular Health

Biomarkers analyzing heart disease risk, arterial function, lipid metabolism, and endothelial integrity.

Examples include:

ApoB

LDL-C, HDL-C, Triglycerides

Lipoprotein(a)

ADMA

CRP-hs

Homocysteine

Omega-3 Index

3.2 Metabolic Health & Blood Sugar

Markers identifying insulin resistance, mitochondrial efficiency, metabolic syndrome, and energy utilization.

Examples include:

Fasting Insulin

Fasting Glucose

HOMA-IR

Triglyceride-Glucose Index (TyG Index)

Hemoglobin A1c

Adiponectin

Leptin

3.3 Liver Health

Evaluates detoxification, fat accumulation, inflammation, and metabolic stress.

Examples include:

ALT

AST

GGT

ALP

Bilirubin

Albumin

Albumin/Globulin Ratio

3.4 Kidney Health

Assesses filtration efficiency, electrolyte balance, hydration, and kidney stress.

Examples include:

Creatinine

eGFR

BUN

Cystatin C

Microalbumin

3.5 Inflammation & Immune Function

Detects chronic inflammation, autoimmune risk, and immune dysregulation.

Examples include:

hs-CRP

ANA

Ferritin

ESR

White Blood Cell Count breakdown

3.6 Hormonal Health

Sex hormones, stress hormones, fertility markers, adrenal function.

Examples include:

Testosterone (Total & Free)

Estradiol

Progesterone

SHBG

DHEA-S

Cortisol

AMH

17-hydroxyprogesterone

3.7 Thyroid Health

Complete thyroid panel often missed in standard testing.

Examples include:

TSH

Free T3

Free T4

Reverse T3

Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies

Thyroglobulin Antibodies

3.8 Nutrient Status

Critical vitamins, minerals, and cofactors that support metabolism, neurological health, and cellular function.

Examples include:

Vitamin D

Vitamin B12

Folate

Iron

Transferrin

Magnesium

Zinc

Copper

3.9 Energy & Mitochondrial Health

Evaluates markers associated with fatigue, low energy, mitochondrial dysfunction.

Examples include:

Lactate

Carnitine

CoQ10 (depending on panel availability)

3.10 Body Composition & Metabolic Risk

Blood indicators related to fat distribution, muscle metabolism, and metabolic strain.

Examples include:

Leptin

Adiponectin

hs-CRP

ALT (overlap with liver)

Triglycerides

3.11 DNA & Longevity Markers

Tracks predispositions and long-term resilience indicators.

Examples include:

Methylation-related signals (depending on membership year)

telomere-associated markers (varies)

(This section depends on specific annual updates.)

=====================================
4. HEALTH CALCULATORS (TOOLS)
=====================================

SOURCE: superpower.com/calculators

Superpower provides medical calculators for interpretation, self-education, and decision support.

A1C → Estimated Average Glucose (eAG)
Converts HbA1c into daily average glucose interpretation.

eGFR Calculator (MDRD)
Estimates kidney filtration efficiency.

Corrected Calcium (Payne Formula)
Adjusts calcium level based on albumin concentrations.

Basal Insulin Calculator
Helps estimate total daily basal insulin needs (Type 1 & 2 context).

=====================================
5. FAQ CORE KNOWLEDGE
=====================================

SOURCE: superpower.com/faqs

This is distilled into LLM-usable bullet points.

5.1 What Superpower Is

A preventive-health membership measuring 100+ biomarkers annually.

Includes clinical team oversight.

Provides lifestyle, nutrition, and health optimization plans.

Not emergency or primary care.

5.2 Testing Process

Blood draw via Quest or mobile phlebotomy.

Takes ~10 days for results.

Some markers require fasting.

5.3 Safety & Compliance

All labs are CLIA/CAP certified.

Tests are physician-authorized.

Members complete informed medical consent during onboarding.

5.4 Availability

Available in the U.S. with some state exclusions.

Members must be over 18.

5.5 Membership Details

Annual renewal.

No hidden fees.

HSA/FSA eligible.

Refund policies depend on blood draw status.

5.6 Medical Team

Provides guidance, interpretation, personalized plans.

Does not treat or diagnose acute diseases.

Does not prescribe medications unless explicitly stated for certain programs.

=====================================
6. KEY SUPERPOWER VALUE PROPOSITIONS
=====================================
6.1 More Data Than Standard Checkups

Typical checkup: 10–20 biomarkers.
Superpower: 100+ biomarkers across every major system.

6.2 Holistic, Multi-System Interpretation

Patterns matter more than individual markers.
Superpower builds longitudinal insight.

6.3 Longevity + Performance Optimization

Supports metabolic flexibility, hormonal balance, mitochondrial function, organ health, and inflammation reduction.

6.4 Personalized Health Plan

Members receive a tailored plan created from biomarker data + goals + lifestyle.

6.5 Preventive, Proactive Approach

Catch issues early rather than waiting for symptoms.

=====================================
7. OTHER SUPERPOWER RESOURCES
=====================================
7.1 Core Pages

Landing/Home – General overview + CTA.

Reviews – Testimonials and scientific validation.

Our Why (Manifesto) – Mission & philosophy: health intelligence for all.

Organizations – Employer health-intelligence programs.

7.2 Blog & Library

Educational content on:

Metabolic Health

Inflammation

Thyroid

Energy

Kidney & Liver

DNA Biomarkers

Heart & Vascular Health

“5 Essential Biomarkers” articles, etc.

Focuses on simple explanations of complex physiology.

7.3 Advanced Panels

Pages detailing:

TyG Index

ANA

AMH

ADMA

ALT

17-hydroxyprogesterone
…and many more.

=====================================
8. COMMUNITY, ACCESS, AND PARTNERS
=====================================
8.1 Partnerships

Comparison pages exist for:

Function Health

Mito Health

Marek Health

InsideTracker

Superpower positions itself as more comprehensive and preventive.

8.2 Access Points

Login / Dashboard

Gift Superpower

Superpower Signals

Careers / Join The Team

Contact: hello@superpower.com

=====================================
9. BRAND PHILOSOPHY (FROM MANIFESTO)
=====================================

Superpower exists because most checkups miss critical markers until disease is already progressing. They aim to expand access to advanced testing typically reserved for elite health clinics or performance programs.

Core beliefs:

Health intelligence belongs to everyone.

Prevention beats treatment.

Data is power.

Your biology is dynamic and should be tracked over time.

Modern life demands better tools than outdated annual checkups.

The brand tone is:

Scientific

Accessible

Empowering

Preventive

Modern

Data-driven
